true.notes: Workshared identity joins include version number as discriminator-gated required context.

notes: Pilot for canonical evidence superset + selectors in the identity domain.
join_hash uses policy-required items only; optional items remain in identity_basis.items for future exploration.
File-local identifiers remain excluded from canonical identity evidence and join-key hashing.
Pareto evidence showed zero fragmentation and zero collisions with name-only in the evaluated population.
This is a label-based join pending availability/export of definition-bearing identity fields for phase filters; treat as provisional.
Policy is provisional pending broader samples; current Pareto evidence shows zero fragmentation and zero collisions for both phase.name and phase.seq.
phase.name is treated as the definition-bearing identifier in current evidence; phase.seq may be added only if future datasets exhibit name collisions.
Accuracy moved to optional - display precision preference, not behavioral identity.
Spec + type + rounding define the unit system (feet-inches, metric, etc.).
Accuracy variance (0.01 vs 1.0) is display formatting, not calculation behavior.
DECISION: Accuracy affects display only, not technical behavior - treat as refinement.
Identity is definition-based via ordered segment (kind,length) sequence; segments are collapsed into line_pattern.segments_def_hash.
line_pattern.segments_def_hash must be derived only from segment kinds and lengths in order and must not incorporate UID, name, or element_id.
Names are labels only and must not participate in joins.
Identity is definition-based: projection weight + color + referenced line pattern definition via pattern_ref.sig_hash.
pattern_ref.kind is too coarse and produces high collisions; it must not be used for joins.
Definition-based identity is structural: ordered grid parameter sequence; reduce to a single derived grids_def_hash for governance joins.
Sparse per-grid scalar keys (e.g., grid[i].shift/angle) are not acceptable for identity due to high collision evidence in Phase-2 Pareto exploration.
target_id/is_solid/grid_count are context/disambiguation only and must not replace grids_def_hash.

Arrow.notes: Arrow styles require arrow-specific geometry + fill/closed flags

Tick.notes: Tick styles require centered/pen weight parameters

Dot.notes: Dot styles share tick parameters for definition

Other.notes: Fallback: include a minimal tick flag to preserve definition

notes: Definition-based identity only; no referential identifiers in joins.
arrowhead.name is label-only and may be null for built-in/system arrowheads.
Downstream domains reference arrowhead identity via this domain signature, not by id or name.
Shape-gated join keys ensure only applicable style parameters are required.

Linear.notes: Linear dimensions must match on witness line control behavior

LinearFixed.notes: LinearFixed dimensions share witness line requirements with Linear

Radial.notes: Radial dimensions must match on center mark configuration

Diameter.notes: Diameter dimensions share center mark requirements with Radial

DiameterLinked.notes: DiameterLinked dimensions share center mark requirements with Radial

Angular.notes: Angular dimensions use common properties only

ArcLength.notes: ArcLength dimensions use common properties only

SpotElevation.notes: Spot dimensions use common properties only

SpotCoordinate.notes: Spot dimensions use common properties only

SpotSlope.notes: Spot dimensions use common properties only

SpotElevationFixed.notes: Spot dimensions use common properties only

notes: Schema v3: Shape-gated join key requirements.
dim_type.shape is now REQUIRED and used as discriminator for shape-specific requirements.
Linear family (Linear, LinearFixed) requires witness_line_control.
Radial family (Radial, Diameter, DiameterLinked) requires center_marks and center_mark_size.
Angular and Spot families use common properties only.
Definition-based tick mark identity via dim_type.tick_mark_sig_hash.
UID-based tick mark fields must never participate in joins.
Policy: color_rgb is definition-bearing.
Policy: arrowhead is definition-bearing only via arrowheads-domain signature hash (text_type.leader_arrowhead_sig_hash), not uid/name/id.
If text_type.leader_arrowhead_sig_hash is unavailable for some records, treat as lower confidence rather than substituting uid/name.
Structured domain: join is performed on vf.def_hash only (definition bundle hash).
vf.def_hash is computed from canonical vf.categories + vf.logic_root + vf.rule_count + ordered vf.rule[###].sig, but those leaf inputs remain identity-only for forensics.
Names/labels and any UID-like identifiers are prohibited from joins for standards governance.
Structured-sequence domain: join identity is the ordered filter-stack definition collapsed into vfa.stack_def_hash.
Leaf stack members and counts are authoritative identity (identity_basis) but must not participate in joins.
Template identifiers are labels/linkage only and are prohibited from joins.
Identity is category-scoped via obj_style.row_key.
Definition includes referenced pattern via obj_style.pattern_ref.sig_hash.
obj_style.weight.projection is required to eliminate observed collisions under row_key + pattern_ref.sig_hash.
Join-key identity captures the override pattern, not where it's used.
vco.baseline_category_path identifies WHICH category is being overridden.
vco.baseline_sig_hash identifies the object_styles baseline definition being overridden.
vco.override_properties_hash collapses all delta properties into a single behavioral signature.
Individual delta properties (line_weight, color, etc.) are forensic detail, excluded from joins.
Structured domain: join identity is the definition bundle hash (def_hash).
def_hash captures complete template behavioral definition including referenced filters and phases.
Names, UIDs, and element IDs are labels/linkage only and must not participate in joins.
category_overrides_def_hash is semantic context but excluded from v1 join for simplicity.
